slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 1 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'are symbols used to connect propositions to form compound propositions, such as AND, OR, and NOT.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Propositional Logic Connectives** is defined as: are symbols used to connect propositions to form compound propositions, such as AND, OR, and NOT. (compound proposition builders).

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'are symbols like Universal (forall) and Existential (exists) that specify the scope of a predicate's variables.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Predicate Logic Quantifiers** is defined as: are symbols like Universal (forall) and Existential (exists) that specify the scope of a predicate's variables. (predicate variable scoping symbols).

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'include Union, Intersection, Difference, and Complement used to manipulate and combine collections of objects.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Set Theory Operations** is defined as: include Union, Intersection, Difference, and Complement used to manipulate and combine collections of objects. (set manipulation mathematical operations).

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a binary relation that is simultaneously reflexive, symmetric, and transitive, partitioning a set into equivalence classes.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Equivalence Relation** is defined as: is a binary relation that is simultaneously reflexive, symmetric, and transitive, partitioning a set into equivalence classes. (partitioning reflexive-symmetric-transitive relation).

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a set combined with a binary relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Partially Ordered Set (Poset)** is defined as: is a set combined with a binary relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. (reflexive-antisymmetric-transitive ordering framework).

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the number of edges incident to a vertex in a graph, with self-loops counting twice.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Vertex Degree** is defined as: is the number of edges incident to a vertex in a graph, with self-loops counting twice. (vertex incident edge count).

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a trail in a finite graph that visits every edge exactly once, existing if exactly zero or two vertices have odd degree.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Euler Path** is defined as: is a trail in a finite graph that visits every edge exactly once, existing if exactly zero or two vertices have odd degree. (single-visit edge traversal trail).

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a closed loop in a graph that visits every vertex exactly once and returns to the starting vertex.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Hamiltonian Cycle** is defined as: is a closed loop in a graph that visits every vertex exactly once and returns to the starting vertex. (single-visit vertex traversal loop).

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a set combined with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Group Theory Group** is defined as: is a set combined with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility. (closure-associativity-identity-inverse algebraic structure).

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is an ordered arrangement of all or part of a set of objects, where the order of selection matters.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Permutation** is defined as: is an ordered arrangement of all or part of a set of objects, where the order of selection matters. (order-sensitive selection arrangement).

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'include Ready, Running, Waiting, New, and Terminated, representing the life-cycle stages of a process.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Process States** is defined as: include Ready, Running, Waiting, New, and Terminated, representing the life-cycle stages of a process. (process lifecycle stage indicators).

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is the process of allocating CPU time to processes in the ready queue using algorithms like Round Robin or SJF.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **CPU Scheduling** is defined as: is the process of allocating CPU time to processes in the ready queue using algorithms like Round Robin or SJF. (ready queue CPU allocation).

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'coordinates the execution of concurrent processes to ensure no two processes access shared data simultaneously.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Process Synchronization** is defined as: coordinates the execution of concurrent processes to ensure no two processes access shared data simultaneously. (prevention of race conditions).

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a strategy that eliminates at least one of the four necessary Coffman conditions (Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait).'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Deadlock Prevention** is defined as: is a strategy that eliminates at least one of the four necessary Coffman conditions (Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait). (coffman condition elimination strategy).

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a resource allocation and deadlock avoidance algorithm that tests for safety by simulating allocation limits.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Banker's Algorithm** is defined as: is a resource allocation and deadlock avoidance algorithm that tests for safety by simulating allocation limits. (safe-state resource allocation simulator).

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a memory management scheme that eliminates the need for contiguous physical memory allocation by dividing logical memory into blocks.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Paging** is defined as: is a memory management scheme that eliminates the need for contiguous physical memory allocation by dividing logical memory into blocks. (elimination of external fragmentation).

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a technique that allows the execution of processes that are not completely in physical memory, using disk storage.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Virtual Memory** is defined as: is a technique that allows the execution of processes that are not completely in physical memory, using disk storage. (logical memory expansion).

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a hardware cache memory that stores recent translations of virtual memory addresses to physical memory addresses.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)** is defined as: is a hardware cache memory that stores recent translations of virtual memory addresses to physical memory addresses. (fast page table lookup cache).

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'decides which memory page to swap out of physical RAM when a new page needs to be allocated (e.g. LRU, FIFO).'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Page Replacement Algorithm** is defined as: decides which memory page to swap out of physical RAM when a new page needs to be allocated (e.g. LRU, FIFO). (physical memory page eviction policy).

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a state where the operating system spends more time swapping pages in and out of disk than executing instructions.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Thrashing** is defined as: is a state where the operating system spends more time swapping pages in and out of disk than executing instructions. (excessive paging performance collapse).

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a node-based binary tree data structure where the left subtree contains keys less than the parent, and the right contains keys greater.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Binary Search Tree (BST)** is defined as: is a node-based binary tree data structure where the left subtree contains keys less than the parent, and the right contains keys greater. (O(log n) average search time).

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'finds the shortest path from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Dijkstra's Algorithm** is defined as: finds the shortest path from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights. (single-source shortest path optimization).

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'solves complex problems by breaking them down into simpler subproblems and storing the results of these subproblems to avoid redundant computing.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Dynamic Programming** is defined as: solves complex problems by breaking them down into simpler subproblems and storing the results of these subproblems to avoid redundant computing. (memoization and tabulation paradigm).

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'maps data of arbitrary size to fixed-size values using a mathematical function, enabling constant-time average insertions and lookups.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Hashing** is defined as: maps data of arbitrary size to fixed-size values using a mathematical function, enabling constant-time average insertions and lookups. (O(1) average lookup complexity).

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property, where the parent node key is compared with its children.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Heap Data Structure** is defined as: is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property, where the parent node key is compared with its children. (efficient priority queue implementation).

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a self-balancing binary search tree where the heights of the two child subtrees of any node differ by at most one.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **AVL Tree** is defined as: is a self-balancing binary search tree where the heights of the two child subtrees of any node differ by at most one. (strictly balanced binary search tree).

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that selects a pivot element and partitions the array around it.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Quick Sort** is defined as: is a divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that selects a pivot element and partitions the array around it. (in-place sorting with O(n log n) average time).

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'represents a graph as an array of linked lists, where each index represents a vertex and its list contains its adjacent neighbors.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Adjacency List** is defined as: represents a graph as an array of linked lists, where each index represents a vertex and its list contains its adjacent neighbors. (space-efficient sparse graph representation).

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'traverses a graph level by level, utilizing a queue data structure to visit vertices in order of increasing distance.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Breadth-First Search (BFS)** is defined as: traverses a graph level by level, utilizing a queue data structure to visit vertices in order of increasing distance. (shortest path in unweighted graphs).

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'traverses a graph by exploring as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking, utilizing a stack.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Depth-First Search (DFS)** is defined as: traverses a graph by exploring as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking, utilizing a stack. (connectivity and topological sorting tool).

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'requires that domain values must be atomic, meaning attributes cannot contain multiple or nested values.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **First Normal Form (1NF)** is defined as: requires that domain values must be atomic, meaning attributes cannot contain multiple or nested values. (atomic column values requirement).

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'requires a table to be in 1NF and ensure that all non-prime attributes are fully functionally dependent on the primary key.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Second Normal Form (2NF)** is defined as: requires a table to be in 1NF and ensure that all non-prime attributes are fully functionally dependent on the primary key. (elimination of partial dependencies).

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'requires a table to be in 2NF and ensure that no non-prime attribute is transitively dependent on the primary key.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Third Normal Form (3NF)** is defined as: requires a table to be in 2NF and ensure that no non-prime attribute is transitively dependent on the primary key. (elimination of transitive dependencies).

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a stronger version of 3NF where for every non-trivial functional dependency X -> Y, X must be a superkey.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF)** is defined as: is a stronger version of 3NF where for every non-trivial functional dependency X -> Y, X must be a superkey. (eliminating overlapping candidate key anomalies).

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'guarantee database transaction reliability: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **ACID Properties** is defined as: guarantee database transaction reliability: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability. (transaction reliability standards).

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a field in a relational database table that uniquely identifies a row in another table, enforcing referential integrity.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Foreign Key** is defined as: is a field in a relational database table that uniquely identifies a row in another table, enforcing referential integrity. (referential integrity constraint).

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a self-balancing search tree structure widely used in databases to enable fast sequential access and range queries.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **B+ Tree Index** is defined as: is a self-balancing search tree structure widely used in databases to enable fast sequential access and range queries. (disk-optimized indexing structure).

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a concurrency control method that guarantees serializability by locking data items in growing and shrinking phases.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Two-Phase Locking (2PL)** is defined as: is a concurrency control method that guarantees serializability by locking data items in growing and shrinking phases. (serializability guarantee in transactions).

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'combines records from two or more tables in a relational database based on a related column between them.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **SQL JOIN** is defined as: combines records from two or more tables in a relational database based on a related column between them. (multi-table data retrieval operation).

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a procedural query language consisting of operations like Select, Project, Join, and Union to manipulate relations.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Relational Algebra** is defined as: is a procedural query language consisting of operations like Select, Project, Join, and Union to manipulate relations. (theoretical query language foundation).

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a conceptual framework of 7 layers that standardizes telecommunication and networking protocol functions.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **OSI Model** is defined as: is a conceptual framework of 7 layers that standardizes telecommunication and networking protocol functions. (7-layer abstraction framework).

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet, emphasizing reliable delivery.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **TCP/IP Protocol** is defined as: is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet, emphasizing reliable delivery. (reliable internet transport framework).

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a 32-bit number that masks an IP address, dividing the IP address into network address and host address portions.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Subnet Mask** is defined as: is a 32-bit number that masks an IP address, dividing the IP address into network address and host address portions. (IP address segmentation tool).

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a standardized exterior gateway protocol designed to exchange routing and reachability information among autonomous systems.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Border Gateway Protocol (BGP)** is defined as: is a standardized exterior gateway protocol designed to exchange routing and reachability information among autonomous systems. (internet autonomous routing protocol).

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'uses the exact same key for both the encryption of plaintext and the decryption of ciphertext.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Symmetric Encryption** is defined as: uses the exact same key for both the encryption of plaintext and the decryption of ciphertext. (single shared-key cryptography).

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'uses a mathematically linked public-private key pair for encryption and decryption operations.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Asymmetric Encryption** is defined as: uses a mathematically linked public-private key pair for encryption and decryption operations. (public-private key pair cryptography).

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'translates human-readable domain names into machine-readable IP addresses.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **DNS (Domain Name System)** is defined as: translates human-readable domain names into machine-readable IP addresses. (internet address resolution directory).

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a protocol used to map an Internet Protocol (IP) address to a physical machine Media Access Control (MAC) address.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)** is defined as: is a protocol used to map an Internet Protocol (IP) address to a physical machine Media Access Control (MAC) address. (IP to MAC address resolver).

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'is a network security device that monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on established security rules.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **Firewall** is defined as: is a network security device that monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on established security rules. (traffic filtering barrier).

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following Computer Science terms is defined as: 'provides 128-bit addresses to replace 32-bit IPv4 addresses, accommodating the massive growth of internet devices.'?
slides.explanation: Correct! **IPv6 Address Space** is defined as: provides 128-bit addresses to replace 32-bit IPv4 addresses, accommodating the massive growth of internet devices. (128-bit internet address space standard).

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 1. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Computer Science Mock Exam 1.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Computer Science questions!
